name: Regedit Started with TrustedInstaller Privileges
notes:
This rule detects regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or with ProcessHacker.exe.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth
SIGMA Title: Regedit as Trusted Installer
SIGMA Description: Detects a regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or by ProcessHacker.exe


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of regedit.exe
when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [processhacker.exe or trustedinstaller.exe]
TD_list: APPLY Regedit Started with TrustedInstaller Privileges on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of regedit.exe
AND when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [processhacker.exe or trustedinstaller.exe]
RED_list: Event name: Regedit Started with TrustedInstaller Privileges
Event description: Detects regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or with ProcessHacker.exe.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
